notes: Brand-stated drop is 5mm but lab measured 9.6mm. Toebox narrow/tapered (70.1mm). Toebox durability 2/5, heel padding durability rated 'Bad'. Outsole durability 'Good'. Midsole softness 39.5 AC (balanced). Flexibility 13.7N. Creel Jacquard Knit upper. Early-Stage Meta Rocker. Supercritical EVA, not PEBA.

Lab-measured values used (brand claims differ: 41.5mm stack, 8mm drop, 8.5% energy return). Forefoot energy return 66.5%, forefoot stack measured separately. Midsole softness 30.3 AC (plush). Traction 0.53 wet-grip. Flexibility 11.2N. Toebox/outsole durability rated qualitatively: toebox 'Bad', outsole 'Good' (not numeric). Heel counter stiffness 'Moderate', torsional rigidity 'Stiff' - no numeric scores given. No plate; subtle forefoot rocker. Jacquard mesh upper.

Drop is lab-measured (11.4mm) vs 10mm claimed. Heel energy return 55.5%, forefoot 61.4%. Air Zoom units in heel and forefoot. Midsole softness 35.2 AC. Toebox width 72.9mm (tapered). Low grip score (0.25), poor wet traction. Flexible (9.4N). True to size. Worse than v40 in cold. Heel counter rated 'Stiff', torsional rigidity 'Flexible' but not given as 1-5 numbers.

Winged nylon plate. Forefoot energy return 73.4%. Midsole softness 38.9 AC (firmer than v3). Slightly small fit, narrow tapered toebox (69.9mm). Neutral shoe. Flexibility 13.5N. Forefoot midsole width 114.7mm, heel 93.4mm. Drop listed as brand 8.0mm, lab 8.7mm.

Neutral daily trainer with mild stability features (reinforced sidewalls, stiff heel counter). Forefoot energy return 62.7%. Midsole softness 41.5 AC (firmer than average). Flexibility 9.6N. Toebox width 72.1mm, height 26.8mm. Fits true to size. Outsole durability rated 'Good' but no numeric durability score given. Cons: non-gusseted tongue, stiff heel counter, low toebox volume.

Lab-measured drop 6.1mm vs brand-stated 5mm. Shock absorption 127 SA. Midsole firm at 40.5 AC. Narrow fit (92.2mm width, 68.9mm toebox). Outsole hardness 76.0 HC, thickness 3.7mm. Stiffness 17.7N. Mild stability suitable for mild overpronation but no motion control features. Meta-rocker design.

PureGEL technology in heel for softer landings. Knit upper, expanded toebox. Midsole softness 39.5 AC (moderate-to-firm). Forefoot stack measured 32mm. Brand weight 304g, brand drop 8mm. Cons: heavy, limited energy return, unpadded tongue. Outsole uses AHAR+ and ASICSGRIP rubber. Surprisingly stable despite high stack. New version: Gel Nimbus 28.

Brand drop 8mm, lab 8.5mm. Midsole softness 19.0 HA. Outsole wear only 0.3mm (best-in-class durability). Tapered toebox (71.4mm) but tall (29.1mm). Wide forefoot midsole (125.1mm). Heel padding durability 4/5, toebox durability 3/5. Subpar breathability.

Neutral daily trainer with new PEBA-based Dreamstrike+ foam; uses EVA 'Support Rods' (not a true plate). No Continental rubber—Adiwear forefoot/heel plus translucent midfoot rubber. Midsole softness 35.0 AC; secondary foam 50.0 AC. Brand-stated drop 10mm, lab 9.7mm; brand stack heel 36mm/forefoot 26mm. Fits slightly small. Rockered geometry, best for heel strikers.

Pebax Wave Plate stops in midfoot (not full-length), so plate_material is Pebax but not carbon/nylon. Midsole softness 23.3 HA. Outsole durability excellent (0.6mm wear, ~500+ miles). Toebox durability scored 2/5, heel padding 5/5. Forefoot traction borderline on wet. True to size. Designed for heel strikers.

Dual-foam midsole: Nitro supercritical foam on top, firmer EVA carrier foam below (36.0 HA). Narrow toebox (70.4 mm) and forefoot. Full-length PumaGrip outsole. Midsole softness 35.8 AC, shock absorption 127 SA, stiffness 14.8N. True to size. Neutral daily trainer; brand drop spec 10mm vs lab 9.3mm.

Brand-stated heel 39mm, forefoot 35mm, drop 4mm vs lab measurements differ. Maximalist daily trainer; narrow midfoot and toebox (92.2mm width, 69.2mm toebox). Heavy at 311g. Outsole hardness 74 HC, outsole thickness 3mm, estimated 300+ miles durability. Midsole softness 41.5 AC. Wide platform (forefoot 126.5mm, heel 100.9mm). Has rocker, semi-gusseted tongue, finger-loop heel tab. Brand release date Jul 15, 2022.

Supercritical nitrogen-infused EVA (DNA Loft v3). Forefoot energy return 58.3%. Midsole softness 20.4 HA (balanced). Knit upper runs warm, not for hot summer runs. Tapered toebox. Rigid heel counter favors heel strikers. Neutral max-cushion daily trainer; GTS version available for stability. Shock absorption 133 SA heel. Flexibility 12.3N.

Maximalist neutral daily trainer. Brand stack 41.5/33.5mm but lab-measured 38/30.2mm. Lab drop 7.8mm (brand 8mm). Soft midsole 18.6 HA. Flexibility 13.2N. Forefoot midsole width 119.4mm, heel 98.9mm. Narrow fit (95.6mm). Shock absorption 133 SA heel/118 SA forefoot. Lackluster breathability and energy return. Heavier weight despite minimal outsole rubber. Suitable for mild pronators despite no medial post.

Full-length PWRRUN PB midsole upgrade. Brand drop 10mm, lab drop 9.7mm. Brand weight 286g matches lab. Neutral daily trainer with some non-intrusive stability features (sidewalls, broad platform). Shock absorption 140 SA, midsole softness 41.7 AC. Outsole durability rated 'Good' qualitatively. Wide width available in select markets. Standard width measured 97.5mm. Best for heel strikers and long easy/moderate runs; falls short at faster paces.

Maximalist stability shoe with PureGEL at heel, 4D Guidance System, non-rockered. Brand drop advertised 10mm vs measured 11.5mm. Midsole softness 35.3 AC. Outsole durability rated 'Decent'. Stability via sidewalls/chassis rather than traditional medial post; has_medial_post not explicitly stated. True to size.

Stability daily trainer with GuideRails support (no medial post). Heel strike oriented, 12mm brand drop. Outsole durability rated good (0.67mm wear); toebox durability poor at 1/5 due to thin engineered mesh/3D Fit Print upper. Midsole softness 19.5 HA. Forefoot felt thin for forefoot strikers.

Moderate stability shoe using midsole walls, sole flares and rocker instead of medial post. PWRRUN+ sockliner. Forefoot energy return 63.5%. Heel SA 127, forefoot SA 122. Midsole softness 22.3 HA. Forefoot midsole width 121.0mm, heel 104.6mm. True to size. Brand drop spec 6mm vs measured 7mm. Flexibility 10.0N.

Mild stability daily trainer using Hoka J-Frame technology instead of a traditional medial post. Firm CMEVA midsole (26.6 HA durometer). Brand drop stated 5mm vs lab 6.3mm. Narrow toebox (70.0mm); Wide version available in select regions. Forefoot energy return 56.1%, forefoot shock absorption greater than heel. Flexibility 17.8N. Outsole durability rating not fully stated in text.

Full-length fiberglass EnergyRods (not a plate). Midsole softness 38.3 AC (Lightstrike 2.0), secondary foam 29.2 AC (Lightstrike Pro). Shock absorption 128 SA. Continental rubber outsole. Toebox durability rated 1/5 (poor upper durability). Fits slightly large. Forefoot landing platform 118.2mm, heel 86.0mm. Designed for midfoot/forefoot strikers.

Super trainer with carbon plate; heel stack 42.5mm exceeds World Athletics 40mm road race limit. ASICS advertises 8mm drop but lab measured 9.6mm. Forefoot energy return 61.8%; midsole softness 31.7 AC (FF Blast+), secondary FF Turbo 33.9 AC. Stiffness 26.5N. Narrow fit (93.6mm), toebox width 74.3mm. Flexible heel counter and poor stability — neutral runners only. Lab weight 237g.

Tempo-oriented carbon-plated trainer; narrow fit and toebox (91.5mm width, 69.7mm toebox). Midsole softness 37.7 AC (Nitro) / 37.1 AC (Nitro Elite). Shock absorption 132 SA. Flexibility 20.6N. Fits slightly small. Outsole durability rated Decent. Not recommended for stability needs. Heel/forefoot midsole widths 82.7mm/109.3mm.

ZoomX = Pebax (ZOTEK PEBA by Zotefoams). Carbon Flyplate sandwiched between dual-density ZoomX (lower 34.5 AC softer, upper 47.2 AC firmer). Air Pods in forefoot. Lab drop 8.5mm (brand 8.0mm). Forefoot energy return 73.9%, heel 74.9%. Shock absorption 144 SA heel/113 SA forefoot. Outsole and toebox durability rated 'Bad'; heel padding 'Good'. Torsional rigidity 'Stiff'; heel counter stiffness 'Flexible'; traction 'Moderate'. Breathability described as best-in-class. Not orthotic friendly, non-removable insole. Strike pattern heel. Slightly small fit, medium width/toebox. Worn by Kelvin Kiptum for marathon WR.

Full-length Energyrods (carbon-infused) act as the plate. Lab drop 8.0mm vs brand-stated 6.5mm; lab forefoot 29.8mm vs brand 33mm. TPEE foam, firmer feel. Shock absorption 136 SA, midsole softness 37.6 AC, stiffness 25.3N. Continental outsole. Toebox durability rated 1/5, but outsole durability rated good. Most stable super shoe per reviewer; not suited for heel strikers. Risk of heel slippage.

Dual-midsole: PWRRUN HG top (33.5 AC) over PWRRUN PB bottom (35.1 AC) with S-shaped carbon plate. Brand lists 8mm drop; lab measured 9.5mm. Stiffness 25.7N (stiff), torsional rigidity rated 'Stiff', heel counter 'Flexible'. Heel SA 139, forefoot SA 103. Best for heel strikers; heavier than v3 (7.3oz). True to size. Stack just under 240g+35mm threshold for racer but qualifies.

Marathon super shoe. Lab heel stack 33.5mm, forefoot 30.8mm, lab drop 2.7mm (brand says 39/34mm, 5mm drop). Forefoot energy return 74.4%. Carbon plate moved higher for snappier toe-off, makes it stiffer (25.3N). Thin 2.0mm outsole, criticized for durability. Heel padding durability rated 5. Page shows conflicting price $250 in text and $500/$250 in tables. True to size, narrow fit.

Fits half size small; narrow toebox. Stiff (80.7N bend test, older methodology). Midsole softness 17.4 HA, outsole hardness 81.6 HC. Toebox durability 1/5, heel padding durability 5/5, outsole durability good (0.5mm indentation). Hoka lists drop as 5mm vs lab 6.6mm. Heel SA 141, forefoot SA 112.

Lab-measured values used for weight (277g M9), drop (3.8mm lab vs 4mm brand), and stacks (insole included). Brand claims heel 33mm/forefoot 29mm. Energy return 53.3% heel / 56.8% forefoot. Vibram outsole, neutral arch, late-stage Meta-Rocker. Narrow fit, not for wide feet. Estimated ~400 mile durability. Price shown as $320 in one spec block but comparison table and brand listing indicate $155 MSRP.

Contragrip outsole, QuickLace system. Lab drop 14.1mm but feels like 8-10mm. Firm midsole (52.9 AC durometer). Designed for muddy/soft ground and cold winter conditions; poor breathability. Toebox durability 4/5, heel padding durability 2/5, outsole durability showed 1.2mm indentation. Outsole hardness 93.9 HC. True to size. No rock plate. Lightest Speedcross yet.

Rock plate in forefoot (material unspecified), plus Trail Adapt system. Lab drop 9.2mm vs brand-claimed 8mm. TrailTack Green rubber, outsole hardness 81.8 HC, outsole wear 0.8mm (good). Toebox durability 2/5, heel padding durability 1/5. Midsole firmness 25.5 HA, firms up 32% in cold. Over 50% recycled upper. Heavier than v16 by 19g. Toebox width wide. Insole removable, orthotic friendly. Not for heavy runners per chart.

Rock plate (material not specified, likely TPU/non-carbon). Lab drop 2.2mm vs brand 4.0mm. Midsole softness 26.0 HA; PWRRUN+ plush insole. PWRTRAC outsole, hardness 92.4 HC. Energy return forefoot 64.5%. 2E width available in select markets. Not for ultras; somewhat firm ride.

Carbon-fiber ENERGYRIM (U-shaped perimeter stiffener) instead of a traditional plate, but provides plate-like stiffness (15.6N). First sub-100g marathon super shoe. Brand-stated stack 39/36mm, drop 3mm; lab measured 38.1/32.2mm. Forefoot energy return 80.2%, heel SA 153/forefoot 142. Extremely soft (21.0 AC). Very narrow fit (89.3mm width, 65.9mm toebox). Continental forefoot rubber replaces liquid rubber. Aggressive forefoot rocker (6.5cm rise). Not for heel strikers; poor durability. Reviewed Apr 2026. Torsional rigidity rated 'Flexible' and heel counter 'Flexible' qualitatively, no numeric 1-5 given.

TPE-based ZoomX foam (not Pebax). Breathability 48 BR (rated Moderate/Low). Torsional rigidity 14.5 Nm, longitudinal stiffness 19.8N (stiff). Outsole wear 0.8mm, outsole thickness 2.9mm, full-length rubber with square lugs. Toebox durability 3/5, heel padding durability 4/5. Snug fit/narrow toebox (94.6mm width, 71.1mm toebox). Forefoot midsole 117mm, heel 99.2mm. Drying potential 54%. Neutral shoe but fairly stable for a max-cushion trainer. Brand drop spec 10mm.

100% Lightstrike Pro midsole, same foam as Adios Pro 3. Plate-free but has a plastic shank in midfoot. Lab measurements differ from brand specs (brand: 38.5/32mm, 6.5mm drop). Continental forefoot outsole. Midsole softness 43.5 AC (firm-ish). Flexibility 9.8N. Shock absorption 147 SA. Toebox durability rated bad; poor laces and short non-gusseted tongue noted. Torsional rigidity described as 'Stiff', heel counter 'Moderate', outsole durability 'Good' (no numeric values given). True to size.

Super trainer with FF Leap top layer over softer FF Blast+ carrier. Lab drop 8.1mm vs brand 8mm. Shock absorption 154 SA heel / 153 SA forefoot. Traction 0.96 (highest tested to date). Torsional rigidity 18.2 Nm despite no plate; rated 'Stiff'. Stiffness moderate (16.5N bend). Heel counter 4/5. Toebox breathability limited. Durability rated Good (outsole/heel) but no numeric rating given. Width medium/medium toebox. True to size.

Max-stack overhaul of Vomero series. Lab stack measurements well below Nike's stated 46mm heel / 36mm forefoot. Dual-foam: ZoomX (training version, denser, 30.5 AC) over ReactX (44.3 AC). Heel SA 147, forefoot SA 116. Heel energy return ~56.9% (forefoot 58.9%). Narrow toebox (69.6mm). Neutral shoe with some stability-friendly features. Stiffness increased 51% over v17. True to size.

Lab drop (9.1mm) differs from Hoka's stated 5mm. Durometer 43.0 AC (firm for max-cushion). Shock absorption 146 SA heel / 133 SA forefoot. Stiffness 16.5N. Midsole width 123.2mm forefoot / 104.2mm heel. Moderate rocker, narrow toebox (72.5mm), good for narrow/average feet. Brand weight 297g. Non-gusseted tongue. Versatile as walking shoe.

Drop brand-listed 4mm, lab measured 3.3mm. Record-breaking shock absorption (152 SA heel, 144 SA forefoot - most cushioned forefoot tested). Midsole softness 27.5 AC (very soft). Torsional rigidity measured 14.7 Nm (moderate/flexible). Stiffness 17.7N. Forefoot midsole width 122.5mm, heel 102.0mm. Toebox width 71.3mm (narrow), gel mold 93.5mm. Outsole thickness 1.9mm, insole 3.5mm. No rocker. Heavy, snug/narrow fit, weak airflow.

Plate-free super trainer despite carbon_racer archetype (plate+stack>=35mm+weight<240g). Brand drop 8mm; lab measured 9.9mm. Forefoot energy return 73.8%. Torsional rigidity measured 20.7 Nm (stiff). Midsole softness 30.8 AC. No wide version; toebox width 74.3mm. Outsole ASICSGRIP rubber but limited coverage and poor toebox durability. Stiffness 18.8N. Heel/forefoot midsole width 95.4/117.9mm.

Lab drop 10.6mm vs brand-stated 8mm; heel stack increased 1.2mm over v4. Forefoot energy return 78.2%, heel 73.2%. Midsole firm 44.5 AC (PWRRUN PB). Torsional rigidity 'moderate' (13.5 Nm). Toebox durability 2/5, heel padding 5/5. Flexibility 12.9N. Forefoot traction 0.52 on wet concrete (SATRA TM144). Width 'medium', toebox width 72.9mm. Versatile tempo/racer; not maximally cushioned.

Plateless super trainer with dual-foam midsole (PEBA-based FF Turbo+ over EVA-based FF Blast+ ECO). Unisex shoe built on men's last (D width). Fits true to size but tight for wide feet. Midsole softness 35.2 AC, secondary foam 41.0 AC. Stiffness 18.4N in 30-deg bend test. Torsional rigidity rated 'Stiff' and heel counter 'Moderate' qualitatively but no numeric values given. Brand-listed weight 8.8 oz/250g. Brand drop 8.0mm. Internal length 270.4mm; toebox width 72.3mm, height 27.7mm.

Lab drop (12.4mm) differs from Hoka's stated 8mm; lab heel stack 44.4mm vs brand-listed 42mm. Uses dated CMEVA foam, no supercritical/PEBA. Notable weight gain (+10%) from v9. Shock absorption 147 SA (high). Midsole softness 34.2 AC (balanced). Toebox width 73.1mm (widened from v9). Outsole durability rated 'decent', toebox/heel padding durability 'good' (not numeric). Rocker present. Marketed as comfort-first daily trainer but archetype inferred as max_cushion given stack>=40mm and heavier weight.

Neutral plush daily trainer. New TPEE-based Infinion supercritical foam (20% EVA), ~29% softer than v14 (21.5 AC). Energy return 65.9% heel / 69.1% forefoot. Torsional rigidity measured 10.3 Nm (flexible). Outsole wear 1.0 mm. Heel padding durability 3/5, toebox durability 4/5. Limited stability—neutral runners only. Stiffness 15.1N. Internal length 273mm, toebox width 73.2mm. Brand drop stated 6mm.

Stability shoe with GuideRails Go To Support system (no traditional medial post). Forefoot energy return 59.2%. Heel SA 138, forefoot SA 101. Midsole softness 34.5 AC (27% softer than v24). Torsional rigidity measured 14.3 Nm; flexibility 14.3N. Brand stated drop 10mm, heel 37mm, forefoot 27mm, weight 300g. Outsole wear only 0.5mm, thickness 4.1mm - excellent durability. Knit upper. True to size.

First shoe RunRepeat tested over 50mm heel and 40mm forefoot stack. TPE-based ZoomX (not PEBA), lower rebound than racers. Features four Air Zoom units (2 heel, 2 forefoot, same as Maxfly 2). Not World Athletics approved. Rocker geometry. Tapered toebox (68.6mm), generous toebox height (30.8mm). Midsole softness 34.5 AC, stiffness 21.5N. Brand-stated drop 10mm, brand heel stack 55mm. Lab measured men's US 9. Shock absorption 173 SA heel / 147 SA forefoot. Forefoot traction 0.51. Torsional rigidity and heel counter described as 'Stiff' but no numeric value.

Brand claims 6mm drop but lab measured 8.6mm. Brand weight 5.9oz/166g (lab 5.85oz/166g), 13% lighter than VF3. Heel SA 137, forefoot SA 94. Midsole softness 34.7 AC, secondary foam 37.8 AC. Stiffness 18.0N. Heel counter described as flexible, torsional rigidity stiff, breathability moderate, outsole durability bad (heel concern for durability). Carbon Flyplate (8 layers, 20deg curvature) heel-to-toe. Toebox width 73.2mm, width 91.7mm (narrower than VF3). Orthotic friendly, removable insole. Best for 5K/10K/half; less ideal for marathon.

Neutral max-cushion shoe; brand-advertised drop is 4mm but lab measured 7.8mm. Brand weight 303g; lab 308g. Midsole softness 25.9 AC (very soft, EVA-based, low energy return). Toebox narrow/restrictive (70.7mm width, 24.4mm height). Outsole durability rated 'Good'; heel padding and toebox durability 'Decent'. Longitudinal stiffness 18.1N. Forefoot midsole width 124.6mm. Heel SA 156, forefoot SA 133. Slightly small fit.

Nitrogen-injected EVA foam (DNA Loft v3), firmer feel at 44.1 AC. GuideRails plus medial post for stability. Toebox sits slightly low; fits slightly small. Outsole wear only 0.8mm (impressive durability); toebox durability 3/5, heel padding durability 4/5. Drop measured by lab (13.5mm) vs brand (12.0mm). Heel stack 121 SA, forefoot 96 SA shock absorption. Flexibility 10.1N. Non-gusseted tongue, no rocker.

Uses Energy Rods 2.0 (five carbon-infused rods) rather than a full carbon plate, so has_plate set false and plate_material none. Brand-stated stack 39/33mm; lab measured 36.6/28.5mm. Softness 29.3 AC, shock absorption 142 SA, torsional stiffness 22.7N (among least rigid super shoes), heel counter rated flexible. Narrow/tapered toebox, poor breathability, durability of new foam noted as ~100 mile window. Highest energy return RunRepeat had tested.

Energy return 60.6% heel / 66.6% forefoot. Heel shock absorption 137 SA, forefoot 110 SA. Midsole softness 41.7 AC (firm-leaning). Torsional rigidity measured 13.6 Nm (around average); flexibility 14.2N. HOKA lists drop as 5mm but lab measured 9.4mm. Toebox durability 2/5 from Dremel test; fits slightly small/narrow. Sticky Rubber outsole improved from 0.49 to 0.73 traction.

Neutral premium daily trainer. Lab energy return 54.1% heel / 57.7% forefoot (below average). Heavy at 303g. Torsional rigidity measured 14.0 Nm (rated moderate, mapped to 3/5). Dual-density DNA Tuned midsole (main 29.2 AC, secondary heel 34.8 AC). Outsole wear only 0.7mm. Drop reduced from 10mm to official 8mm. Fits slightly small. Toebox durability 3/5. Forefoot traction 0.57.

Forefoot energy return 74.2%, heel 67.5%. Torsional rigidity measured 7.8 Nm (rated flexible). Brand spec: 10mm drop, 36/26mm stack, 245g. Single-slab Nitro foam (vs dual-foam in v3). Midsole softness 41.8 AC. Fit runs narrow (toebox 71.6mm); true to size per 60 votes. PumaGrip outsole, herringbone pattern. Non-rockered. Outsole wear 1.1mm. Not recommended for pronators; midsole firms up in cold.

Super trainer with ZoomX (Invincible 3/Pegasus Plus formulation, not Alphafly) plus secondary SR-02 EVA foam. Forefoot energy return 67.0%, heel 61.0%. Brand drop claimed 8mm, lab 9.6mm. Brand stack 40/32mm. Stiffness 21.5N (relatively flexible for a carbon plate). Narrow fit (90.9mm width, 72.6mm toebox). Neutral; called stable but not recommended for stability needs. Heel SA 144, forefoot 111. Midsole softness 36.5 AC, secondary foam 33.6 AC. Has forefoot rocker.

Stability trainer using 4D Guidance System and PureGEL instead of medial post. Brand drop 8mm, lab measured 9.3mm. Torsional rigidity measured 20.2 Nm (rated 'Stiff'). Midsole softness 41.3 AC (firmer). Shock absorption 133 SA heel / 116 SA forefoot. Stiffness 16.9N. Toebox durability 4/5, heel padding durability 4/5. Non-rockered design. Width measured 96.6mm. Criticized for low energy return and price.

Full-length Energy Arc carbon plate with concave design over open midsole channel; midsole groove traps pebbles. Brand drop 6mm vs lab 7.3mm; brand stacks 41/35mm. Midsole softness 21.8 AC (main), secondary foam 27.5 AC. Flexibility 25.1N (stiff). Shock absorption 144 SA heel / 118 SA forefoot. Torsional rigidity rated 'Stiff', heel counter 'Moderate' (numeric not given). Fits slightly small. FANTOMFIT upper. Toebox/upper durability noted as weak. Archetype daily_trainer (stack<38mm, not <240g).

Forefoot energy return 58.1%. Drop measured 12.2mm vs 10mm claimed. Torsional rigidity measured 12.2 Nm (flexible), no 1-5 score given. Midsole softness 40.5 AC (slightly firm). Non-rockered, flexible (12.3N bend). Toebox tapered/narrow (70.6mm). Heel padding durability 4/5. Outsole carried over unchanged from Ghost 17.

EVA-based midsole, modest energy return. Drop is 4.2mm lab vs 6mm brand spec. Tapered/narrow toebox (69.8mm) despite medium overall width (95.4mm). Neutral shoe with stability improvements via raised sidewalls and firmer foam (28.5 AC). Heavier than v13 (264g). Heel stack 37mm lab vs 38mm brand. Shock absorption heel 142 SA, forefoot 128 SA. Toebox durability 4/5. 5cm toe spring rocker.

Lab-measured values used (brand-stated: 47mm heel, 41mm forefoot, 6mm drop). Maximalist daily trainer, plateless. Shock absorption high (148 SA heel, 138 SA forefoot) but energy return low. Midsole softness 31.9 AC (yellow) / 31.3 AC (white). Flexibility 18.3N (stiff). True to size. Toebox medium width. Cons: heavy, low bounce, poor rubber coverage, high price. Outsole durability noted as a concern but no 1-5 rating given.

Stability-focused super shoe with full-length forked carbon plate (4 curved prongs). Forefoot energy return 75.8%. Torsional rigidity measured 15.8 Nm and 30-deg bend 26.1N (very stiff). Midsole softness 33.3 AC (PB) / 29.8 AC (HG). Shock absorption 137 SA heel / 107 SA forefoot. Heavier than most super shoes; aggressive Speedroll rocker, pronounced heel flare. Toebox narrow (69.3mm); fits slightly small. Outsole durability rated Good. Width/fit 95.2mm.

Nitrogen-infused DNA Loft v3, softer formulation (33.8 AC durometer). Brooks advertises 6mm drop but lab measured 7.3mm. Heel stack lab 38.5mm; brand-claimed heel 39.0mm. Neutral shoe but very stable due to wide base (forefoot 119.1mm, heel 101.5mm). Stiff (18.7N flex), torsional rigidity 17.3Nm. Outsole rubber 3.3mm thick, only 0.8mm wear. Energy return low (heel 51.3%, forefoot 50.5%). Best for midfoot/forefoot strikers; weak heel shock absorption. Toebox durability 3/5. Non-rockered. Weight is a drawback. Score breakdown: daily 89, tempo 42, race 30, stability 90.

Lab-measured values used (heel 38.5mm, forefoot 28.2mm, drop 10.3mm, weight 293g). DNA Tuned dual-density nitrogen foam (forefoot 30.0 AC softer, heel 37.6 AC firmer). Toebox height low at 23.6mm. Shock absorption 133 SA heel/109 SA forefoot. Stiffness 15.0N. Toebox durability 3/5, heel padding durability 4/5, outsole durability rated 'Good'. Heel midsole width 89.7mm, forefoot 117.2mm. Fits true to size. Subtle rocker, no plate.

foam_name: ZoomX / ReactX / full-length Air Zoom unit
notes: Super trainer with triple-layer midsole (ZoomX top, ReactX, full-length Air Zoom unit replacing carbon plate). Nike claims 10mm drop; lab measured 11.8mm. Brand weight 309g, lab 308g. Brand stack heel 45.0/forefoot 35.0mm. Narrow fit (91.3mm width, 70.3mm toebox); widths listed as Narrow/Normal/Wide in comparison table but review states no wide sizes available. Stiffness 21.2N (stiff), torsional rigidity rated 'Stiff', heel counter 'Stiff' but no numeric out-of-5 given. Outsole durability 'Decent', toebox durability 'Bad'. Shock absorption heel 155 SA, forefoot 136 SA. Midsole softness 36.9 AC; ReactX 39.9 AC. Strike pattern heel; rocker present but mild.

Forefoot energy return 56.5%. Torsional rigidity measured 12.6 Nm (flexible). Flexibility 10.9N. Midsole softness 36.2 AC (balanced). Toebox tapered/narrow (69.6mm width, 24.8mm height). Neutral shoe noted as very stable due to wide base (forefoot midsole 116.8mm, heel 95.4mm). Shock absorption 140 SA heel, 100 SA forefoot. Toebox durability 3/5. Widths labeled Narrow/Normal/Wide/X-Wide = B/D/2E/4E depending on country.

PureGEL heel insert. Brand-stated heel stack 43.5mm and drop 8mm differ from lab (39.5mm heel, 5.2mm drop). Hybrid ASICSGRIP+AHAR outsole. Midsole softness 38.8 AC, flex 23.1N (stiff), torsional rigidity 20.5 Nm (stiff). Energy return forefoot 45.9%. Drying potential 48%. Knit/engineered mesh upper. Neutral trainer with early forefoot rocker.

Maximalist neutral daily trainer with PureGEL insert. Midsole softness 39.3 AC (firmer than expected). Stiffness 20.2N. Heel strike pattern, true to size. Marketed neutral but very supportive (reinforced sidewalls, wide platform). Low energy return is main drawback. Brand drop 8.0mm, brand weight 305g. Outsole/heel padding/toebox durability rated 'Good/Decent' qualitatively (no numeric).

Energy return: 80.4% heel, 82.6% forefoot. Forefoot energy return notably high. Stiffness 23.1N (very stiff), midsole softness 36.1 AC, secondary foam 33.7 AC. Shock absorption 148 SA heel / 124 SA forefoot. Extended PWRPLATE carbon plate with decoupled midsole. Torsional rigidity rated 'Moderate', heel counter 'Flexible' (not numeric). Outsole/toebox durability rated 'Bad' (poor durability). Width fit 92.9mm, toebox 72.3mm. True to size. Brand drop 8mm, brand weight 170g. UMass study: up to 3.6% running economy improvement. Highly unstable, not for heel strikers.

PWRPLATE is a sculpted carbon-based plate. Lab measurements used (drop brand 8.0mm). Torsional rigidity measured 16.7 Nm (rated 'moderate' but no 1-5 score given); stiffness 21.1N. Medial heel rubber removed - durability concern for heel strikers. Toebox durability 1/5. Shock absorption: heel 148 SA, forefoot 118 SA. Midsole softness 37.0 AC (balanced). Toebox width 74.8mm. Neutral racing shoe; narrow heel (78.8mm) limits stability.

Lab drop 10.7mm vs brand 8mm. Energy ARC carbon plate, Ultra Light VPU outsole. Torsional rigidity measured 12.5 Nm (flexible, no 1-5 rating given). Narrow heel reduces stability; neutral runners only. Energy return forefoot 74.9%. Toebox width 74.5mm; overall width 93.8mm. Heel SA 144, forefoot SA 112.

Supercritical nitrogen-infused TPU foam (Enerzy NXT). Knit upper. Heel SA 158, forefoot SA 129. Midsole softness 33.3 AC (soft). Flexibility 12.8N. Toebox width 73.4mm, toebox height 27.0mm, width 93.7mm. Forefoot midsole width 113.5mm, heel 94.7mm. Light rocker, orthotic friendly, removable insole, all seasons. Heel padding durability 4/5. Firms up in cold. Not ideal for heel strikers or those needing stability. Foam confirmed as TPU under microscope. Weight scored sub-240g qualifying lightweight but no plate, so classified max_cushion due to stack>=38mm.

Pebax ZoomX midsole (37.8 AC main, 37.7 AC secondary). Spoon-shaped FlyPlate carbon plate. Brand-claimed drop is 8mm but lab measured 11.1mm; brand weight 184g, lab weight 190g. Shock absorption 131 SA heel / 89 SA forefoot. Flexibility 18.4N. Toebox width 73.1mm. Best for heel strikers and marathoners with narrow/medium feet; less ideal for 5K/10K and wide feet. True to size. Rocker present. Not orthotic friendly, non-removable insole.

Vibram Megagrip outsole. Brand weight 297g, brand drop 4.0mm, brand heel/forefoot stack 33.0/29.0mm. Fits half size small. Shock absorption 131 SA heel / 124 SA forefoot. Midsole softness 29.7 AC. Stiffness 18.2N (90-deg bend). Forefoot midsole width 116.9mm, heel 94.1mm. Toebox durability 4/5; toebox width 73.4mm, height 26.7mm. Rocker geometry. Awards: top pick trail and stability running shoes. Durability value reflects toebox durability rating.

Dual-density midsole: softer PWRRUN PB core (42.3 AC) wrapped by firmer EVA-based PWRRUN frame (48.5 AC). Mild-stability shoe with firm frame for support, no medial post. Brand-stated drop is 8mm but lab measured 10.1mm. Forefoot energy return 72.2%. Torsional rigidity measured 13.7 Nm (rated 'Moderate'). Stiffness 15.1N. Toebox and heel padding durability both 2/5; outsole durability rated 'Good'. Narrow fit (92.5mm width, 70.8mm toebox). XT-900 outsole rubber. Heel SA 123, forefoot SA 108.

Stack is below 35mm but classified as a 5K/10K super shoe with carbon plate and ultra-low 128g weight. Energy return: 76.3% heel / 77.9% forefoot. Forefoot traction 0.49 (SATRA TM144). Flexibility 7.0N to 30deg. Midsole softness 31.9 AC primary, 31.0 AC secondary ZoomX. Toebox width 66.7mm, width 89.7mm, toebox height 23.1mm. Fits slightly small. Narrow toebox, low drop unsuitable for heel strikers. Outsole durability rated 'Decent', toebox durability 'Bad'. No rocker, no removable insole, summer season.

Super trainer competing with Adidas EVO SL; firm ride (40.2 AC durometer) and aggressive SpeedRoll rocker may divide opinions. Energy return 70.6% heel / 74.7% forefoot. Shock absorption 137 SA heel / 122 SA forefoot. Torsional rigidity measured 14.5 Nm (rated 'Moderate'). Flexibility 16.6N. Toebox durability scored 2/5. Wide forefoot midsole (123.4 mm) gives strong stability. XT-900 rubber outsole. Stability score 90, tempo 92, race 89.

Mild stability shoe using new H-Frame stability system (firmer secondary foam 44.1 AC) instead of J-Frame. Main foam softer EVA at 35.8 AC. Forefoot energy return 60.9%. Brand-listed drop is 8mm but lab measured 11.3mm. Early-Stage Meta-Rocker. Torsional rigidity measured 19.5 Nm. Stiffness 18.3N. Outsole durability indentation 0.5mm (excellent). Brand weight 9.0oz/256g. No supercritical foam. Poor breathability/grip. has_medial_post set false as it uses H-Frame, not traditional medial post.

Dual-foam setup: FF Leap (A-TPU, 31.9 AC) under plate, FF Turbo+ (PEBA, 38.0 AC) closer to foot. Flat full-length carbon plate. Stiffness 21.1N in 30-deg bend. Brand-stated drop 5.0mm, lab 6.0mm. Forefoot stack 32.7mm, heel 38.7mm. Shock absorption 147 SA heel / 122 SA forefoot. Narrow fit (90.5mm width, 70.1mm toebox) but generous toebox height (30.6mm). Unstable for heel strikers; best for mid/forefoot strikers. Lab weight 163g, brand 170g. Width and toebox durability rated bad; heel padding good; outsole decent. Torsional rigidity described as 'Stiff', heel counter 'Flexible' (no numeric scores given).

Dual-foam: firmer FF Turbo (PEBA-based) over softer FF Blast+. Outsole AHARPLUS rubber, outsole wear only 0.6mm (good durability) but toebox durability 1/5 and heel padding durability 2/5. Medial-side reinforcement for stability; very wide platform (forefoot 121.8mm, heel 96.6mm). Stiff (19.6N bend). Narrow fit, no wide sizes. New version: Superblast 3. Notable: stack exceeds 40mm WA legal limit. Outsole thickness 2.6mm.

Lab drop 10.6mm vs brand-stated 6mm. Secondary foam DNA Flash v2 beneath nylon SpeedVault plate. Forefoot energy return 66.2%; forefoot stack also 35.0mm. Midsole softness 25.7 AC (top), 40.1 AC (secondary). Torsional rigidity measured 21.4 Nm (very high). Bend/stiffness 17.0N. Heel counter stiffness rated Moderate, torsional rigidity Stiff (qualitative). True to size. SpeedVault plate has cutouts; RapidRoll rocker. Only standard width available.

Neutral max-cushion daily trainer; lab drop 9.9mm vs Brooks-claimed 6mm. Midsole softness 39.2 AC (firmer than typical supercritical foams). Best for forefoot/midfoot strikers; minimal heel bevel. GlideRoll Rocker. Toebox tapered (71.0mm). Stiffness 20.0N. Heel/forefoot shock absorption 138/121 SA. Outsole durability rated 'Good' qualitatively but no numeric value given.
